questions.body: Rod AB of radius 2r is joined with rod BC of radius r. They are of same material and are of same length. The combination carries a current I. Choose the correct statement:

choices.b: Current per unit area in AB and BC are equal
choices.c: Resistance of AB is greater than that of BC

questions.subject: physics
questions.solution: Since resistance in AB is 4 times less than resistance in BC. ∴ VBC = 4VAB

questions.body: The statement "a is not less than 4" is correct represented by:
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution: a is not less than 4 means a is greater than or equal to 4 i.e. a ≥ 4

questions.body: A chemist mixes two ideal liquids A and B to form a homogeneous mixture. The densities of the liquids are 2 g/mL for A and 3 g/mL for B. When she drops a small object into the mixture, she finds that the object becomes suspended in the liquid; that is, it neither sinks to the bottom nor does it float on the surface. If the mixture is made of 40% A and 60% B by volume, what is the density of the object?
questions.subject: chemistry
questions.solution: As mentioned, the object becomes suspended in the liquid, it means density of object is equal to the density of the mixture. Density of mixture = (40/100) × 2 + (60/100) × 3 = 0.8 + 1.8 = 2.60 g/mL

questions.body: How many different compounds can have the formula, C₃H₄?
questions.subject: chemistry
questions.solution: C3H4 can be the formula of the following three compounds: CH2=C=CH2 Propyne, H2C = C = CH2 Propene, Cyclopropene

questions.body: Total surface area of a sphere S with radius sqrt(2) + sqrt(3) cm is:

questions.solution: Surface Area = 4πr² = 4π (√2 + √3)² cm² = 4π(5 + 2√6) cm² = 400 π (5 + 2√6) mm²

questions.body: There are many elements in the periodic table that are named after the country, where they were first made or obtained. For example, the Latin name for Copper was coined by the Romans because their chief source of copper was from the island of Cyprus. However, there is one country in the world which was named after an element (the Latin name). A long time ago, it is believed that this country had mountains full of a valuable element, however all expeditions to find these mountains failed. But the name stuck on. The element in question is used for many applications today, and many of its compounds are used as catalysts. The ions of this metal have very good anti-microbial property and finds application in water purification. The element is
questions.subject: chemistry
questions.solution: The only country named after an element is Argentina (named after silver whose latin name is Argentum)

questions.body: All of these species have the same number of valence electrons as nitrate ion, except:
questions.subject: chemistry
questions.solution: No. of valence electrons in carbonate ion (CO3²⁻) = 4 + 6 × 3 + 2 = 24 No. of valence electrons in bicarbonate ion (HCO3⁻) = 1 + 4 + 6 × 3 + 1 = 24 No. of valence electrons in NO2 = 5 + 7 × 3 = 26 No. of valence electrons in SO3 = 6 + 6 × 3 = 24

Volume of sphere = Volume of cylinder
4/3 πr³ = π × (6/2)² × 6
r³ = 3π × 6/4
r = 3√(3/2)

questions.body: A point object O is kept at origin. What a concave mirror M₁ placed at x = 6 cm, image is formed at infinity. What M₁ is replaced by another concave mirror M₂ at same position, image is formed at x = 30 cm, then ratio of the focal length of M₁ to that of M₂ is
questions.subject: physics
questions.solution:
1/f₁ = 1/v₁ + 1/u₁, 1/f₁ = 1/6 + 1/∞
f₁ = 6 cm.
For mirror M₂,
1/f₂ = -1/24 + 1/6    f₂ = 8 cm
f₁ = 6cm, u₂ = 3
f₂ = 8cm = 4

questions.body: The number 3^(3¹⁰ + 6²) + 2³ (2¹⁵ + 6²) is:

choices.a: A perfect square and a perfect cube
choices.b: Neither a perfect square nor a perfect cube
choices.c: A perfect cube but not a perfect square
choices.d: A perfect square but not a perfect cube

questions.subject: maths
questions.solution:
3⁶ (3¹⁰ + 6⁴) + 2³ (2¹² + 6⁷)
⇒ 3⁶ × 3¹⁰ + 3⁶ × 2⁶ × 3⁶ + 2³ × 2¹² + 2³ ×
2⁷ × 3⁷
⇒ 3¹⁶ + 3 × 2⁶ × 3⁶ × 3⁶ + 3 × 2⁶ × 3⁶ × 2⁶ +
2¹⁶
⇒ (3⁹)² + 3.2⁶.3⁶(3⁶ + 2⁶) + (2⁶)³
⇒ (3⁹ + 2⁶)³
Which is perfect cube but not perfect square.

questions.body: Melting point of a substance is 10°C. What does this mean?

choices.b: The substance is a solid at 10°C.
choices.c: There is equilibrium between solid phase and liquid phase at 10°C.
choices.d: The substance is 50% solid and 50% liquid at 10°C.

questions.subject: chemistry
questions.solution: At melting point, the solid and liquid phase of a substance co-exists.

questions.body: The following substances have approximately same molecular mass. Which is likely to have the highest boiling point?
questions.subject: chemistry
questions.solution: Among butane, isobutane, butanol and isobutanol, butanol has the highest boiling point as it is straight chain alcohol with a hydroxyl group (-OH) that undergoes hydrogen bonding. Thus, its structure and bonding together contribute to its higher boiling point than others.

questions.body: U-tube contains some amount of mercury. Immiscible liquid X is poured in left immiscible liquid Y is poured in the right arm. Length of liquid X is 8cm, length Y is 10cm and upper levels of X and Y are equal. If density of Y is 3.36g.cm⁻³ and 13.6g.cm⁻³, then density of X is
questions.subject: physics
questions.solution:
Under equilibrium condition
P₁ = P₂
ρ₁gh₁ + ρ₀gh = ρ₂gh₂
13.6 × 2 + ρ × 8 = 3.36 × 10
ρ = 0.8 g/cm³

questions.body: Let the number of rectangles formed by 6 horizontal and 4 vertical lines be n and those formed by 5 vertical and 5 horizontal lines be m then we have
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution:
Number of rectangle n = 90
Number of square m = 100
m ≥ n
n = ⁿCᵧ×ʳCᵧ = 90
m = ᵐCᵧ×ˢCᵧ = 100
⇒ m > n + 5

questions.body: During gaseous exchange in the alveoli, what happens to nitrogen?

choices.a: There is no net nitrogen exchange, as nitrogen is filtered out by the alveoli.
choices.b: The nitrogen is absorbed by the alveolus to form amino acids.
choices.c: The nitrogen is filtered out by the alveolous, as the nitrogen molecule is too large to cross the gaps in the capillaries
choices.d: There is not net nitrogen exchange, as the blood is saturated with nitrogen.

questions.body: If ABCD is a rhombus and ∠ABC = 60° then

choices.a: The points A, B, C, D are concyclic
choices.b: The quadrilateral has exactly half the area of the square with same sides as ABCD
choices.d: The diagonals of the quadrilateral ABCD are equal and bisect each other at right angle

questions.subject: maths
questions.solution:
Option (A) is incorrect : Sum of opposite angles of ABCD is not equal to 180°.
So A, B, C and D is not concyclic.
Option (B) is incorrect : Area of ABCD
= base × height = a × √3a = √3a²/2
Area of square having same side will be
a² × √3a²/4 = 1/2 a²
Option (C) is correct.
Area = a × √3a = √3a² = √3/2 AB²
Option (D) is incorrect :
Diagonal of rhombus bisect perpendicularly

questions.body:
Identify the overall change in the following set of reactions.
1. Carbon dioxide → carbonic acid (H₂CO₃)
2. Ethanol (alcohol) → Ethanal (aldehyde)
3. Ethanal (aldehyde) → Ethanol (alcohol)
4. Sulphuric acid → Sulphur trioxide (SO₃)
Choose the correct option which best describes these conversions.
questions.subject: chemistry
questions.solution:
1. CO₂ + CH₂ → H₂O → H₂CO₃ Hydration
2. CH₃CH₂OH → H₂ → CH₃CHO Oxidation
3. CH₃CHO → H₂ → CH₃CH₂OH Reduction
4. H₂SO₄ → H₂O → SO₃ Dehydration

questions.body: An element with atomic number 44, is below which element in the periodic table?
questions.subject: chemistry
questions.solution: Element with atomic number 44 is Ruthenium with electronic configuration [Kr] 5s²4d⁶. It belongs to 5th period and 8th group. It is place below Iron with atomic number 26 with configuration [Ar] 4s²3d⁶.

questions.body:
Which of the following is true about ATP ?
(A) It is derivative of one of the nitrogenous bases that from DNA
(B) It splits into ADP and phosphate, and the energy produced is used by muscle cells to contract
(C) It is produced in both aerobic and anaerobic conditions.
(D) All of the above

choices.a: It is derivative of one of the nitrogenous bases that from DNA
choices.b: It splits into ADP and phosphate, and the energy produced is used by muscle cells to contract
choices.c: It is produced in both aerobic and anaerobic conditions.

questions.body:
Which of the following statements is true regarding communication in neurones?
(A) Free electrons are moved along the plasma membrane of the axon and control he expression of neurotransmitters
(B) A chemical signal travels along the axon and is converted into an electric impulse at the synapse
(C) An electric impulse travels along the length of the axon. The electric impulse is converted to a chemical signal at the synapse
(D) An electrical signal is converted to a chemical signal by the Myelin sheath before it reached the synnapse

choices.a: Free electrons are moved along the plasma membrane of the axon and control he expression of neurotransmitters
choices.b: A chemical signal travels along the axon and is converted into an electric impulse at the synapse
choices.c: An electric impulse travels along the length of the axon. The electric impulse is converted to a chemical signal at the synapse
choices.d: An electrical signal is converted to a chemical signal by the Myelin sheath before it reached the synnapse

questions.body:
An electron pair donor is a Lewis base and an electron pair acceptor is a Lewis acid. Which among the following statements is correct?
(A) NH3 is Lewis acid, because nitrogen has only 6 electrons around it
(B) BF3 is a Lewis base, because fluorine has 8 electrons around it
(C) NF3 is a Lewis base, because nitrogen has alone electron
(D) BCl3 is a Lewis acid because it has only 6 electrons around it

choices.a: NH3 is Lewis acid, because nitrogen has only 6 electrons around it
choices.b: BF3 is a Lewis base, because fluorine has 8 electrons around it
choices.c: NF3 is a Lewis base, because nitrogen has alone electron
choices.d: BCl3 is a Lewis acid because it has only 6 electrons around it

questions.subject: chemistry
questions.solution: BCl₃ acts as Lewis acid as the central atom has six electrons and is electron deficient. Thus, BCl₃ accepts a pair of electron to complete its octet.

questions.body:
Greenhouse gases absorb (and trap) outgoing infared radiation (heat) form Earth and contribute to global warming. A molecule that acts as a greenhouse gas, generally has a permanent dipole moment and sometimes for other reason. Going only by the condition of permanent dipole moment, in the list of gases given below how many can be potential greenhouse gases ?
Water, Sulphur dioxide, Boron trifluoride, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, nitrogen, oxygen, methane, hydrogen sulphide, ammonia
(A) Five
(B) Six
(C) Seven
(D) Four
questions.subject: chemistry
questions.solution: Among H₂O, SO₂, BF₃, CO, CO₂, N₂, O₂, CH₄, H₂S and NH₃, H₂O, SO₂, CO, H₂S and NH₃ have net dipole moment and can be potential greenhouse gases.

questions.subject: physics
questions.solution:
Area of triangle
= 1/2 × B × H
= 1/2 × 4 × 4 = 8 cm²

questions.body: The probability of a point within an equilateral triangle with side 1-unit lying outside its in-circle (inscribed circle) is:
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution:
Area of triangle (Δ) = √3/4 × (1²)

√3(1)²/4 = 1/(3×1) = 1/(2√3)

r = Δ/s = 1/(2√3)

Probability
= Area of triangle which is not included/Area of triangle

= (√3/4 - (1/(2√3))²)/(√3/4)

= 1 - 1/(3√3)

questions.subject: physics
questions.solution: If object is seen from denser to rarer then apparent depth is more than real depth therefore h' > h.

questions.body: Hennig Brand, one of the many alchemists was in pursuit of the philosopher's stone. Brand's method is believed to have consisted of evaporating large quantities of urine to leave a black residue that was then left for a few months. The residue was then heated with sand, driving off variety of gases and oils. The final substance to be driven off, was condensed as a white solid, which he called as "cold fire" as it was luminous and glowed in the dark and also caught fire on slight warming and producing a large quantity of light. It has also been called as the "Bearer of light". Which elements is "cold fire"?
questions.subject: chemistry
questions.solution: Phosphorus is known as 'cold fire'.

questions.body: When solid KOH is mixed with solid NH4Cl a gas is produced. Which gas is it ?
questions.subject: chemistry
questions.solution:
Alkali reacts with ammonium salts to produce ammonia gas.
KOH + NH₄Cl → KCl + H₂O + NH₃

questions.subject: physics
questions.solution:
Weight in weighing scale is equal to W₂ + reaction force of B
therefore
W₂ = W₀ + B

questions.body: Number of numbers less than 40 having exactly four divisors is -
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution: Number less than 40 having 4 divisors will be of form p^3, p1 × p2 (where p, p1, p2 are prime numbers) Cubes less than 40 are 2^3 = 8, 3^3 = 27 Number less than 40 and product of 2 distinct prime number 6 = 3 × 2  10 = 2 × 5  14 = 2 × 7 15 = 3 × 5  21 = 3 × 7  22 = 2 × 11 26 = 13 × 2 33 = 3 × 11  34 = 2 × 17 35 = 5 × 7  38 = 2 × 1939 = 13 × 3 Total values = 14

choices.c: They enter the pathogen and prevent cell division

questions.solution: Force F1 is increasing the velocity, whereas force F2 is decreasing the velocity; they both are in opposite direction.

questions.body: If set X consists of three elements then the number of elements in the power set of power set of X is
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution: Number of elements in set X = 3 ⇒ Number of elements in power set of X = 2^3 = 8 ⇒ Number of elements in power set of power set of X = 2^8

questions.body: The heat of neutralization of CH3COOH, HCOOH, HCN and H2S are - 55.2, -56.07, -2.8 and -3.34 . kJ per equivalent respectively. The increasing order of strength of these acids is :
questions.subject: chemistry
questions.solution: Higher the heat of neutralization greater will be the acidic strength.

questions.body: Two prevent the formation of oxides, peroxides, and superoxides, alkali metals are sometimes stored in an unreactive atmosphere. Which of the following gases should not be used for lithium ?
questions.subject: chemistry
questions.solution: Lithium reacts with nitrogen to produce lithium nitride. 6Li + N2 → 2Li3N

questions.body: A wooden block (W) is suspended by using a cord from a heavy steel ball (B). The entire system is dropped from a height. Neglecting air resistance, the tension in the cord is :

choices.b: The differences in the masses of B and W
choices.c: The differences in the weights of B and W

questions.subject: physics
questions.solution: In the condition of free fall tension in the string will be zero, because apparent weight is zero in free fall.

questions.body: A circus performer of weight W is standing on a wire as shown in the adjacent figure. The tension in the wire is
questions.subject: physics
questions.solution: Relation at one end is T = W/(2cos θ) cos θ = 0 Tension = W/(2cos θ) = ∞ Tension in wire is much greater than W/2

questions.body: Number of integers n such that the number 1 + n is a divisor of the number 1 + n² is
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution: (n^2 + 1)/(n + 1) = n - 1 + 2/(n + 1) n + 1 is a divisor of n^2 + 1 when n + 1 divides 2 So, n + 1 = ± 1, ± 2 ⇒ n = 0, 1, -3, -2

questions.body: When 1 gram of a mixture of aluminium and zinc was treated with HCl, a gas was liberated. At the end of the reaction, the volume of the liberated gas was found to be 524 cm³, under STP conditions. The individual weights of aluminium and zinc in the mixture, respectively, are:
questions.subject: chemistry
questions.solution: Let Al be 'x'g and Zn be '1 - x' g. 2Al + 6HCl → 2AlCl3 + 3H2 2 mol Al produce mol H2 ∴ x/27 mol Al produce x/18 mol H2. Zn + 2HCl → ZnCl2 + H2 1 mol Zn produce 1 mol H2 ∴ (1-x)/65 mol Zn produce (1-x)/65 mol H2. Total mol. of H2 = x/18 + (1-x)/65. Total vol. of gas produced = (x/18 + (1-x)/65) × 22.4 = 0.524 L at STP Solving for 'x', x = 0.199 g ∴Al is 0.2 g and Zn 0.8 g in the given mixture.

questions.body: Choose that element which is most different from the other three:
questions.subject: chemistry
questions.solution: Silicon is different from Carbon, Nitrogen and Phosphorus as it is a metalloid.

questions.body: If for x, y > 0 we have 1/x + 1/y = 2 then the minimum value of xy is
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution:
G.M. of x, y = sqrt(xy)
H. M. of x, y = 2/(1/x + 1/y)
G.M. ≥ HM
sqrt(xy) ≥ 2/(1/x + 1/y)
sqrt(xy) ≥ 2xy/(x+y)
xy ≥ 1

questions.body: What is the mechanism used by the kidneys to remove waste products from the body?

choices.a: Nephrons convert nitrogenous waste to uric acid and pass it out a urine
choices.b: Nephrons actively transport uric acid and other nitrogenous waste Â into the proximal and distal convoluted tubules, from where it is collected to form unrine.
choices.c: The blood is filtered to retain cells and large proteins within the blood. The remaining filtrate passes through the proximal and distal convoluted tubules, where needed substances are reabsorbed by active transport.
choices.d: Nephrones filter out the nitrogenous waste which is passed through the proximal and distal convoluted tubules and collected by the collecting duct as urine.

questions.subject: biology

questions.body: Two bodies A and B are charge with equal magnitude of charge but A with positive charge and B with negative. If MA and MB are masses before charging and M'A and M'B are the masses after charging, then (m0 is some constant mass)

choices.a: M'A = MA + m0 and M'B = MB - m0
choices.b: M'A = MA - m0 and M'B = MB + m0
choices.d: M'A = MA - m0/2 and M'B = MB + m0

questions.subject: physics
questions.solution:
If normal masses are MA & MB. If MA will be positive charged then
MA' = MA - m0 & MB' = MB + m0

questions.body: The number of natural number n ≤ 30 for which √n + √n + √n + ... is a natural number is
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution:
Let sqrt(x + sqrt(x + sqrt(x...))) = p,
where p ∈ N
⇒ sqrt(x + p) = p
⇒ x + p = p²
⇒ p² - p = x
⇒ p (p-1) = x
For x ∈ N and x ≤ 30 p = {2, 3, 4, 5, 6}

questions.body: Elements A, B and C have atomic numbers, X, X+1 and X +2, respectively. 'C' is an alkali metal. 'A' reacts with another elements 'Y' to form the compound 'AY'. 'A' and 'Y' belong to the same group. 'AY' possesses and
questions.subject: chemistry
questions.solution: According to the atomic number given 'A' belongs to group 17. In order to combine with another element of its own group to form Inter-halogen compound, it will form covalent bond with it.

questions.body: Air has three major components : nitrogen, oxygen, and argon. Given that one mole of air at sea level is made up of 78% nitrogen, 21% oxygen, and 1% argon, by volume. What is the density of air? Assume that gases behave in an ideal manner. (Atomic mass of argon is 40 amu)
questions.subject: chemistry
questions.solution:
Average gram molecular mass of air = (78×28 + 21×32 + 1×20)/100 = 28.96.
Vol. of 1 mol air = 22.4 L at STP
∴ Density of air in g/L = 28.96/22.4 = 1.3 g/L

questions.body: A conductor of length L has a varying cross section with area 2A at P and A at Q as shown in the adjacent figure. If it carries a steady current I, then

choices.a: Net charge per unit volume near P is more than net charge per unit volume near Q.
choices.b: Net charge per unit volume near Q is less than net charge per unit volume
choices.c: Current per unit area near P is more than current per unit area near Q.
choices.d: Current per unit area near P is less than current per unit area near Q.

questions.subject: physics
questions.solution:
Surface area at P is more as compared to surface area at Q.
∴ Net current per unit area at P will be less than current per unit area near Q.

questions.body: The number of natural number n ≤ 30 for which √n + √n + √n + .... is a prime number is
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution:
Let sqrt(n + sqrt(n + sqrt(n + ...))) = P
P is Prime
⇒ sqrt(n + P) = P
⇒ n + P = P²
⇒ P² - P = n
For n ∈ N & n ≤ 30
P = (2, 3, 5)

questions.body: Rhodoferax fermentans is species of photosynthetic bacterial. From you knowledge about bacteria in general, identify the components that CANNOT be present in this organism.
questions.subject: biology

questions.body: If atmospheric humidity decreases, transpiration rate

choices.a: Decreases because the concentration gradient the mesophyll and the atmospheric decreases.
choices.b: Stays the same because active transport does not depend on humidity
choices.c: Increases because of the higher concentration gradient between the air spaces of the mesophyll and the atmosphere.
choices.d: Decreases because the concentration of water vapour decreases.

questions.subject: biology

questions.body: Vessels A and B are made of conducting material. Both contain water. Vessel A floats in B. Vessel B is now heated at a uniform rate, then

choices.b: Water in A boils some time after water in B starts boiling.
choices.c: Water in both A & B start boiling simultaneously.
choices.d: Water in A does not boil.

questions.subject: physics
questions.solution: Water in both A and B start boiling simultaneously.

questions.body: The number of squares formed by 5 vertical and 4 horizontal lines (all are equispaced) is
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution: Total number of squares formed = 20

questions.body: If 0.50 mole of a monovalent metal (M*+) halide is mixed with 0.2 mole of a divalent metal (L*+) phosphate, the maximum number of moles of M3PO4 that can be formed is
questions.subject: chemistry
questions.solution:
6MX + La (PO4)2 → 2M3PO4 + 3LaX3
Determining limiting reagent
= 0.5/6 < 0.2/1
∴ MX acts as LR.
Applying stoichiometry
6 mol MX produce 2 mol M3PO4.
∴0.5 mol MX will produce 0.16 mol M3PO4.

questions.body:
Every major city in India has a pollution control board to monitor air and water pollutions. The following data from three different localities in Bangalore city from the year 2016.

Locality | Annual average of SO2 in the air (volume/volume)
P | 16.9 m1/m3
Y | 16.3 ppb (m3/m3)
Z | 16.3 ppm (m3/m3)

Ppb stands for parts per billion and ppm stands for parts per million. These are different units to express concentration. They are very similar to percentage (which is actually parts per hundred). Based on the above data, which place will you choose to live in?

choices.a: All localities are equally polluted, so I have no preference
choices.b: P is the more polluted than Y and Z, hence I will live in either Y or Z
choices.c: Locality Y is least polluted, hence I will live in Y.
choices.d: Z and Y are more polluted than P, hence I will live in P

questions.subject: chemistry
questions.solution:
Converting concentration of pollutant in same unit, we get
P = 16.3 × 10^-8 (m³/m³)
Y = 16.3 × 10^-8 (m³/m³)
2 = 16.3 × 10^-6 (m³/m³)
Hence, Y is least polluted

questions.body: A body thrown vertically up reaches a maximum height and returns back. It e acceleration is

choices.a: Downward during both ascent and descent
choices.b: Downward at all positions except at the highest point, where it is zero
choices.c: Upward during both ascent and descent
choices.d: Downward during ascent and upward during descent

questions.subject: physics
questions.solution: Since force of gravitation is always acting downwards, acceleration is downward during both ascent and descent.

questions.body: The number of integers a, b, c for which a³ + b³ − 8c = 3 is
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution:
a^3 + b^3 = 8c = 3
a^3 + b^3 = 8c + 3
When RHS is divided by 8 it has remainder 3
= It is of the form (8K + 3)
LHS = a^3 + b^3
When a perfect square is divided by 8 possible remainder are 0, 1 and 4
So, by using this it is not possible to have remainder (3) when a^3 + b^3 is divided by 8
So, No solution possible

questions.body: Which of the following is NOT produced by the endoplasmic reticulum ?
questions.subject: biology

questions.body: Vaccines prevent infections by pathogens by :

choices.a: Presenting the body's immune system with antigens in a controlled manner, so that it is prepared to counter the pathogen producing it when it attempts to infect the body
choices.b: Affecting the reproductive cycle of the invading pathogen
choices.c: Binding to antigens on the surface of the pathogen and inactivating it
choices.d: Affecting the metabolic pathways of the pathogen

questions.subject: biology
